definitions.neurotransmitters: Chemical substances produced by neurons that underlie behavior.
definitions.blood-brain barrier: A firewall-like structure that regulates the passage of nutrients into the brain.
definitions.synaptic vesicles: very tiny spheres clustered at the presynaptic terminal with hollow centers into which approximately 10,000 molecules of a typical neurotransmitter can be stored for later release from a neuron.
definitions.synapse: The junction at which two neurons communicate via the release of a neurotransmitter molecule.
definitions.ionotropic receptors: Not explicitly defined in this chunk.
definitions.ligand-gated transmembrane ion channels: ionotropic receptors that open or close in response to ligand binding, allowing ions to travel through.
definitions.metabotropic receptors: Not explicitly defined in this chunk.
definitions.reuptake: The reabsorption of a neurotransmitter via a protein transporter located on the (pre-synaptic) membrane of the axon terminal.
definitions.autoreceptor: A special type of protein receptor located on the membranes of nerve cells that is sensitive to the neurotransmitters released by the neuron on which it sits.

examples: Neurons in the substantia nigra contain iron, neurons in the hippocampus contain zinc, neurons in the locus coeruleus contain copper, and neurons in the red nucleus contain cobalt.
GABA and glutamate are simple amino acids and are the most abundant neurotransmitters in the human brain.

a large group of evolutionarily related proteins that activate G-proteins and secondary messengers.
